name: Guan Di
description: Guan Di, also known as Guan Yu, is a revered figure in Chinese culture, elevated to the status of a deity associated with war, righteousness, loyalty, and wealth. Originally a historical general who lived during the late Eastern Han dynasty (c. 160 – 220 AD), Guan Yu distinguished himself through his unwavering loyalty to his sworn brother Liu Bei and his exceptional military prowess. His legendary exploits, including his heroic defense of Liu Bei's territories and his unwavering commitment to justice, were later embellished in folklore and historical romances such as the Romance of the Three Kingdoms. Over time, Guan Yu's image transformed from that of a historical warrior to a divine protector worshipped by people from all walks of life. He is often depicted with a red face, a long black beard, and a green robe, wielding his signature weapon, the Green Dragon Crescent Blade. Temples dedicated to Guan Di can be found throughout China and in Chinese communities worldwide, where he is venerated as a guardian deity who brings prosperity, protects against evil, and upholds justice. His enduring popularity reflects the deep-seated values of loyalty, integrity, and courage that are highly esteemed in Chinese culture. He is also considered a patron saint of policemen, security guards and triad societies. Guan Di's influence extends beyond religious worship, shaping ethical principles and inspiring individuals to embody his virtues in their daily lives. His deification represents a profound cultural phenomenon, transforming a historical figure into a symbol of moral excellence and divine protection.
subtitle: God of War, Righteousness, and Wealth

keyMyths.title: Oath of the Peach Garden
keyMyths.description: Guan Yu, Liu Bei, and Zhang Fei swore an oath of brotherhood in a peach garden, pledging lifelong loyalty to one another. This cemented their bond and set the stage for their later exploits.

keyMyths.title: Journey of a Thousand Miles
keyMyths.description: After being captured by Cao Cao, Guan Yu made a journey of a thousand miles to rejoin Liu Bei, demonstrating his unwavering loyalty. He famously refused to betray his oath.

keyMyths.title: Death and Deification
keyMyths.description: Guan Yu was eventually captured and executed. After his death, stories of his spirit helping people spread, leading to his deification and widespread worship as Guan Di.

sources.author: Luo Guanzhong
